sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation found in sources

special_mechanics.name: Climber
special_mechanics.description: Lemmings climb vertically but fall down if they hit an overhang

special_mechanics.name: Floater
special_mechanics.description: Uses a parachute to fall safely from heights, preventing fall damage

special_mechanics.name: Bomber
special_mechanics.description: Explodes after a five-second timer, destroying itself and destructible landscape in close proximity

special_mechanics.name: Blocker
special_mechanics.description: Stands still and prevents other lemmings from passing; other lemmings reverse direction when hitting a Blocker

special_mechanics.name: Builder
special_mechanics.description: Builds a stairway of 12 steps, stops when hitting head, running out of bricks, or hitting solid object

special_mechanics.name: Basher
special_mechanics.description: Tunnels horizontally through destructible terrain, cannot break through steel barriers

special_mechanics.name: Miner
special_mechanics.description: Tunnels diagonally downwards through destructible terrain, cannot break through steel barriers

special_mechanics.name: Digger
special_mechanics.description: Tunnels directly downwards through destructible terrain, cannot break through steel barriers

tier_notes: Tier 2: Lemmings is a puzzle-strategy game where player controls lemming abilities rather than direct character movement. No quantitative physics parameters (velocities, accelerations, gravity values) are documented in available sources. Game mechanics are qualitatively described through skill system documentation. Sources contain only high-level gameplay descriptions, level data, and regional differences, with no technical movement parameters.
